title: Validate run_id before prompt interpolation in BaseStage.run()
analysis:
run_id is accepted as a bare `str` parameter in BaseStage.run() (line 88 of base.py) and directly f-string-interpolated into the reconciliation context prompt on lines 115 and 121. project_id is validated on line 93 via validate_project_id(), but run_id has no equivalent guard — an asymmetry the task description calls out.

In practice run_id is always str(uuid4()) from harness.py:368 or targeted.py:90, so the risk is latent, not active. The fix adds character-set validation (regex `^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$`) to reject anything that could break out of the intended prompt structure.

The existing validation.py has two patterns: validate_* (returns error dict, for MCP handlers) and require_* (raises ValueError, for internal code). BaseStage.run() uses the validate_* style with manual raise, so we follow that exact pattern for consistency.

The test_stages.py TestProjectIdValidation class (lines 399-627) provides the exact pattern to mirror: same _patch_stage helper, same mock_deps fixture, same watermark/stage setup. We add a parallel TestRunIdValidation class with tests for empty, whitespace-only, injection characters, and valid UUID-format strings.

steps.description: Write unit tests for validate_run_id in validation.py: test that empty string returns error dict, whitespace-only returns error dict, strings with injection characters (newlines, quotes, braces, backticks, semicolons) return error dict, and valid UUID-format strings (hex + hyphens) return None. Also test underscores and plain alphanumeric are accepted.

Add validate_run_id(run_id: str) -> dict[str, str] | None to validation.py. Use re.fullmatch(r'[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+', run_id) after a non-empty check. Return error dict with 'error' and 'error_type' keys on failure, None on success — matching the validate_project_id pattern exactly.

Write TestRunIdValidation class in test_stages.py mirroring TestProjectIdValidation: test_run_raises_on_empty_run_id (empty string), test_run_raises_on_whitespace_run_id (spaces), test_run_raises_on_injection_run_id (string with newline or backtick), test_run_allows_valid_uuid_run_id (standard uuid4 string passes and stage completes successfully), test_recon_context_includes_run_id (captured payload contains the run_id value).

Import validate_run_id in base.py and add the guard `if err := validate_run_id(run_id): raise ValueError(err['error'])` immediately after the project_id validation block (after line 94), before watermark consistency checks. This mirrors the project_id guard on line 93.
Run the full test suite (pytest fused-memory/tests/test_stages.py) to confirm all new and existing tests pass together. Verify no regressions in TestProjectIdValidation or other test classes.

design_decisions.decision: Use regex ^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$ rather than UUID-specific format validation
design_decisions.rationale: The goal is prompt injection prevention, not format enforcement. A permissive safe-character allowlist blocks all injection vectors (newlines, quotes, backticks, braces, semicolons) while remaining forward-compatible if run_id generation changes from uuid4 to another safe format. This matches the task description's suggestion.

design_decisions.decision: Add validate_run_id (returns error dict) not require_run_id (raises directly)
design_decisions.rationale: BaseStage.run() already uses the validate_* + manual raise pattern for project_id (line 93-94). Consistency within the same call site is more important than adding a require_* shorthand. Both styles already coexist in validation.py.

design_decisions.decision: Place run_id validation after project_id validation, before watermark checks
design_decisions.rationale: Fail fast on both identity parameters before doing any cross-checks or payload assembly. project_id and run_id are both interpolated into the prompt, so validating them adjacently keeps the safety guards grouped.
Reusing the same _patch_stage / mock_deps / MemoryConsolidator pattern ensures the tests are consistent with existing conventions and the test class is immediately familiar to maintainers.

reuse.what: validate_project_id pattern (error dict return, non-empty check)
reuse.how: validate_run_id follows the exact same signature and return convention — returns dict[str, str] | None with 'error' and 'error_type' keys
The walrus-operator guard `if err := validate_run_id(run_id): raise ValueError(err['error'])` is structurally identical to the project_id guard

TestRunIdValidation reuses the same _fake_assemble_payload, _fake_run_stage_via_cli, mock_deps fixture, and _patch_stage context manager pattern. Can inherit or duplicate — duplication preferred for test isolation
